steps.title: Read the blank in context
steps.content:
The first sentence establishes that Al-Malaika used traditional forms. The second sentence says that even in this superficially traditional work, she was breaking new ground. The blank must connect those two ideas — the traditional form and the ground-breaking content — with a contrast signal.
  • Choice A — therefore,: Signals cause-and-effect, implying the ground-breaking content results from the traditional form. That relationship isn't in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — in fact,: Emphasizes or confirms the previous statement rather than contrasting it. Doesn't show the tension between traditional form and innovative content. ✗
  • Choice C — moreover,: Adds more of the same, continuing in the same direction. The passage needs a turn, not an addition. ✗
  • Choice D — though,: A concessive contrast word meaning "even so" or "despite that." Correctly signals that despite the traditional form, Al-Malaika was breaking new ground. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The blank needs a word that says despite the traditional surface, something innovative was happening. Only "though" captures that concessive contrast. That is the correct answer.
